records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.278 - It is reported that The Prophet (sw) had seventeen gray hairs in his head and beard.
records.arabicText: 278 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ كَانَ فِي رَأْسِهِ وَ لِحْيَتِهِ سَبْعَ عَشْرَةَ شَيْبَةً.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.279 - The Prophet, Imam Hussain ibn Ali (as) and Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) used to dye their hair with katam (a plant-based dye often used in combination with henna to produce a darker color).
records.arabicText: 279 - وَ كَانَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ وَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَخْتَضِبُونَ بِالْكَتَمِ.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.280 - And Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as) used to dye his hair with both henna and Al-Katam (plant-based black)."
records.arabicText: 280 - وَ كَانَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَخْتَضِبُ بِالْحِنَّاءِ وَ اَلْكَتَمِ.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.281 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Dyeing (the hair or beard) with black brings comfort to women and inspires awe in enemies."
records.arabicText: 281 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اَلْخِضَابُ بِالسَّوَادِ أُنْسٌ لِلنِّسَاءِ وَ مَهَابَةٌ لِلْعَدُوِّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.281 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Dyeing (the hair or beard) with black brings comfort to women and inspires awe in enemies."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.282 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) commented on the verse of Allah (swt), the Exalted, "And prepare for them whatever force you can muster" (Surah Al-Anfal; 8:60).
Imam (as) said: "Among it is dyeing with black”.
(From Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) );
A man entered upon the Messenger of Allah (sw) with his beard dyed yellow (light color), and the Messenger of Allah (sw) said to him, "How good this is."
Then he entered again after that with his beard dyed with henna (reddish color), and the Messenger of Allah (sw) smiled and said: "This is better than the previous."
Then he entered again after having dyed it black, and the Prophet smiled and said:
"This is better than both of the previous”.
records.arabicText:
282 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ تَعَالَى «۝ وَ أَعِدُّوا لَهُمْ مَا اِسْتَطَعْتُمْ مِنْ قُوَّةٍ ۝»
قَالَ «مِنْهُ اَلْخِضَابُ بِالسَّوَادِ
وَ إِنَّ رَجُلاً دَخَلَ عَلَى رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ قَدْ صَفَّرَ لِحْيَتَهُ فَقَالَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ «مَا أَحْسَنَ هَذَا»
ثُمَّ دَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ بَعْدَ هَذَا وَ قَدْ أَقْنَى بِالْحِنَّاءِ فَتَبَسَّمَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَقَالَ «هَذَا أَحْسَنُ مِنْ ذَاكَ» ثُمَّ دَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ وَ قَدْ خَضَبَ بِالسَّوَادِ فَضَحِكَ إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَ «هَذَا أَحْسَنُ مِنْ ذَاكَ وَ ذَاكَ».
records.thaqalaynMatn:
A man entered upon the Messenger of Allah (sw) with his beard dyed yellow (light color), and the Messenger of Allah (sw) said to him, "How good this is."
Then he entered again after that with his beard dyed with henna (reddish color), and the Messenger of Allah (sw) smiled and said: "This is better than the previous."
Then he entered again after having dyed it black, and the Prophet smiled and said:
"This is better than both of the previous”.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.283 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"A woman should not neglect herself, even if it means wearing a necklace around her neck, Nor should She leave her hands without dyeing, even if it is by lightly applying henna, even if She is elderly."
records.arabicText: 283 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لاَ يَنْبَغِي لِلْمَرْأَةِ أَنْ تُعَطِّلَ نَفْسَهَا وَ لَوْ أَنْ تُعَلِّقَ فِي عُنُقِهَا قِلاَدَةً وَ لاَ يَنْبَغِي لَهَا أَنْ تَدَعَ يَدَهَا مِنَ اَلْخِضَابِ وَ لَوْ أَنْ تَمْسَحَهَا بِالْحِنَّاءِ مَسْحاً وَ إِنْ كَانَتْ مُسِنَّةً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.283 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A woman should not neglect herself, even if it means wearing a necklace around her neck, Nor should She leave her hands without dyeing, even if it is by lightly applying henna, even if She is elderly."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.284 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"When nails are affected by Nuura (depilatory paste), they can change their appearance, such that they may resemble the nails of the deceased. Therefore, there is no harm in changing (their appearance)."
records.arabicText: 284 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنَّ اَلْأَظَافِيرَ إِذَا أَصَابَتْهَا اَلنُّورَةُ غَيَّرَتْهَا حَتَّى أَنَّهَا تُشْبِهُ أَظَافِيرَ اَلْمَوْتَى فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِتَغْيِيرِهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.284 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When nails are affected by Nuura (depilatory paste), they can change their appearance, such that they may resemble the nails of the deceased. Therefore, there is no harm in changing (their appearance)."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.285 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said to Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as):
"O’ Ali, spending a dirham on dyeing is better than spending a thousand dirhams in other paths for the sake of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious. It has fourteen benefits: it repels odors from the ears, improves eyesight, softens the nostrils, freshens breath, strengthens the gums, removes ailments, reduces the whispering of Satan, brings joy to the angels, gladdens the believer, enrages the disbeliever, it is a form of adornment, a fragrance, it makes Munkar and Nakir (the angels who question the dead in their graves) feel shy, and it is a source of innocence (purity) in one's grave”.
records.arabicText: 285 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ لِعَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «يَا عَلِيُّ دِرْهَمٌ فِي اَلْخِضَابِ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ أَلْفِ دِرْهَمٍ فِي غَيْرِهِ فِي سَبِيلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ فِيهِ أَرْبَعَ عَشْرَةَ خَصْلَةً يَطْرُدُ اَلرِّيحَ مِنَ اَلْأُذُنَيْنِ وَ يَجْلُو اَلْبَصَرَ وَ يُلَيِّنُ اَلْخَيَاشِيمَ وَ يُطَيِّبُ اَلنَّكْهَةَ وَ يَشُدُّ اَللِّثَةَ وَ يَذْهَبُ بِالضَّنَى وَ يُقِلُّ وَسْوَسَةَ اَلشَّيْطَانِ وَ تَفْرَحُ بِهِ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةُ وَ يَسْتَبْشِرُ بِهِ اَلْمُؤْمِنُ وَ يَغِيظُ بِهِ اَلْكَافِرَ وَ هُوَ زِينَةٌ وَ طِيبٌ وَ يَسْتَحِي مِنْهُ مُنْكَرٌ وَ نَكِيرٌ وَ هُوَ بَرَاءَةٌ لَهُ فِي قَبْرِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.285 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said to Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "O’ Ali, spending a dirham on dyeing is better than spending a thousand dirhams in other paths for the sake of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Glorious. It has fourteen benefits: it repels odors from the ears, improves eyesight, softens the nostrils, freshens breath, strengthens the gums, removes ailments, reduces the whispering of Satan, brings joy to the angels, gladdens the believer, enrages the disbeliever, it is a form of adornment, a fragrance, it makes Munkar and Nakir (the angels who question the dead in their graves) feel shy, and it is a source of innocence (purity) in one's grave”.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.286 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"I indeed shave (the head) every Jumu’ah, from one washing to the next."
records.arabicText: 286 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنِّي لَأَحْلِقُ فِي كُلِّ جُمُعَةٍ فِيمَا بَيْنَ اَلطَّلْيَةِ إِلَى اَلطَّلْيَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.286 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I indeed shave (the head) every Jumu’ah, from one washing to the next."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.287 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said to a man:
"Shave (the head) for it enhances your beauty."
records.arabicText: 287 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ لِرَجُلٍ: «اِحْلِقْ فَإِنَّهُ يَزِيدُ فِي جَمَالِكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.287 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said to a man:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Shave (the head) for it enhances your beauty."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.288 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Shaving the head outside of Hajj or 'Umrah is a form of humiliation for your enemies and a source of beauty for you."
The meaning of these words the Prophet became apparent, when He described the Khawarij, saying:
"They will exit from the religion as an arrow exits from the bow, and their sign is shaving (the head) completely.
records.arabicText:
288 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «حَلْقُ اَلرَّأْسِ فِي غَيْرِ حَجٍّ وَ لاَ عُمْرَةٍ مُثْلَةٌ لِأَعْدَائِكُمْ وَ جَمَالٌ لَكُمْ.
وَ مَعْنَى هَذَا فِي قَوْلِ اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: حِينَ وَصَفَ اَلْخَوَارِجَ فَقَالَ «إِنَّهُمْ يَمْرُقُونَ مِنَ اَلدِّينِ كَمَا يَمْرُقُ اَلسَّهْمُ مِنَ اَلرَّمِيَّةِ وَ عَلاَمَتُهُمُ اَلتَّسْبِيدُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.288 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Shaving the head outside of Hajj or 'Umrah is a form of humiliation for your enemies and a source of beauty for you."
The meaning of these words the Prophet became apparent, when He described the Khawarij, saying:
"They will exit from the religion as an arrow exits from the bow, and their sign is shaving (the head) completely.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.289 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Removing hair from the nose enhances the appearance of the face."
records.arabicText: 289 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَخْذُ اَلشَّعْرِ مِنَ اَلْأَنْفِ يُحَسِّنُ اَلْوَجْهَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.289 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Removing hair from the nose enhances the appearance of the face."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.290 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Washing the head with Al-khatmi (a type of herbal shampoo or a plant known as marshmallow root) every Jumu’ah provides protection from vitiligo and insanity."
records.arabicText: 290 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «غَسْلُ اَلرَّأْسِ بِالْخِطْمِيِّ فِي كُلِّ جُمُعَةٍ ' أَمَانٌ مِنَ اَلْبَرَصِ وَ اَلْجُنُونِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.290 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Washing the head with Al-khatmi (a type of herbal shampoo or a plant known as marshmallow root) every Jumu’ah provides protection from vitiligo and insanity."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.291 – (Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) ) states:
"Washing the head with Al-khatmi (a type of herbal shampoo or marshmallow root) removes poverty and increases sustenance."
records.arabicText: 291 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «غَسْلُ اَلرَّأْسِ بِالْخِطْمِيِّ يَنْفِي اَلْفَقْرَ وَ يَزِيدُ فِي اَلرِّزْقِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.291 – (Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) ) states:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Washing the head with Al-khatmi (a type of herbal shampoo or marshmallow root) removes poverty and increases sustenance."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.292 - In another report, (Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) ) states:
"Washing the head with Al-khatmi (a type of herbal shampoo or marshmallow root) is a remedy and a form of refreshment”.
records.arabicText: 292 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «غَسْلُ اَلرَّأْسِ بِالْخِطْمِيِّ نُشْرَةٌ.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.293 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Washing the head with Al-khatmi (a type of herbal shampoo or marshmallow root) removes dirt and purifies impurities”.
records.arabicText: 293 - قَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «غَسْلُ اَلرَّأْسِ بِالْخِطْمِيِّ يَذْهَبُ بِالدَّرَنِ وَ يُنَقِّي اَلْأَقْذَاءَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.293 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Washing the head with Al-khatmi (a type of herbal shampoo or marshmallow root) removes dirt and purifies impurities”.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.294 - And when the Messenger of Allah (sw) was distressed, Archangel Jibril (as) instructed him to wash his head with sidr (lote tree leaves), and this sidr was from the lote tree of Sidrat al-Muntaha (the utmost boundary in the heavens)."
records.arabicText: 294 - وَ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اِغْتَمَّ فَأَمَرَهُ جَبْرَئِيلُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنْ يَغْسِلَ رَأْسَهُ بِالسِّدْرِ وَ كَانَ ذَلِكَ سِدْراً مِنْ سِدْرَةِ اَلْمُنْتَهَى.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.295 - Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said:
"Washing the head with sidr (lote tree leaves) brings sustenance abundantly."
records.arabicText: 295 - وَقَالَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «غَسْلُ اَلرَّأْسِ بِالسِّدْرِ يَجْلِبُ اَلرِّزْقَ جَلْباً».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.295 - Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Washing the head with sidr (lote tree leaves) brings sustenance abundantly."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.296 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Wash your heads with sidr (lote) leaves, for every Muqarrab Angel and every Mursil Prophet has sanctified it. Whoever washes their head with sidr leaves, Allah (swt) will remove the whisperings of Shaytan (Satan) from them for seventy days. Whoever is free from the whisperings of Shaytan for seventy days will not disobey Allah (swt), and whoever does not disobey Allah (swt) will enter Paradise."
records.arabicText: 296 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اِغْسِلُوا رُءُوسَكُمْ بِوَرَقِ اَلسِّدْرِ فَإِنَّهُ قَدَّسَهُ كُلُّ مَلَكٍ مُقَرَّبٍ وَ كُلُّ نَبِيٍّ مُرْسَلٍ وَ مَنْ غَسَلَ رَأْسَهُ بِوَرَقِ اَلسِّدْرِ صَرَفَ اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ وَسْوَسَةَ اَلشَّيْطَانِ سَبْعِينَ يَوْماً وَ مَنْ صَرَفَ اَللَّهُ عَنْهُ وَسْوَسَةَ اَلشَّيْطَانِ سَبْعِينَ يَوْماً لَمْ يَعْصِ اَللَّهَ وَ مَنْ لَمْ يَعْصِ اَللَّهَ دَخَلَ اَلْجَنَّةَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.296 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Wash your heads with sidr (lote) leaves, for every Muqarrab Angel and every Mursil Prophet has sanctified it. Whoever washes their head with sidr leaves, Allah (swt) will remove the whisperings of Shaytan (Satan) from them for seventy days. Whoever is free from the whisperings of Shaytan for seventy days will not disobey Allah (swt), and whoever does not disobey Allah (swt) will enter Paradise."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.297 - Imam Hasan ibn Ali (as) came out of the bathhouse, and a man met him and said:
"May your (backside) bathing be good."
Imam (as) said: "O’ fool, what business do you have mentioning the ‘backside’ here ?"
(The man) replied: "May your bath be pleasant"
Imam (as) said: "And if my bath is pleasant, what benefit is that to me?"
(The man) replied: "May your warm water be pleasant."
Imam (as) said: "Woe to you! Do you not know that 'Hamim' (hot water) is sweat?"
(The man) asked Him: “So what should I say?”
Imam (as) said: "Say: 'May what is purified from you be pleasant, and may what is pleasant from you be purified.'".
records.arabicText:
297 - وَ خَرَجَ اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مِنَ اَلْحَمَّامِ
فَقَالَ لَهُ رَجُلٌ طَابَ اِسْتِحْمَامُكَ فَقَالَ لَهُ «يَا لُكَعُ وَ مَا تَصْنَعُ بِالاِسْتِ هَاهُنَا»
فَقَالَ طَابَ حَمَّامُكَ قَالَ «إِذَا طَابَ اَلْحَمَّامُ فَمَا رَاحَةُ اَلْبَدَنِ مِنْهُ»
فَقَالَ طَابَ حَمِيمُكَ فَقَالَ «وَيْحَكَ أَ مَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ اَلْحَمِيمَ اَلْعَرَقُ»
قَالَ لَهُ كَيْفَ أَقُولُ قَالَ «قُلْ طَابَ مَا طَهُرَ مِنْكَ وَ طَهُرَ مَا طَابَ مِنْكَ.

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.298 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"If your brother says to you after you come out of the bath, 'May your bath be pleasant,' then reply with, 'May Allah (swt) grant you well-being.'"
records.arabicText: 298 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا قَالَ لَكَ أَخُوكَ وَ قَدْ خَرَجْتَ مِنَ اَلْحَمَّامِ طَابَ حَمَّامُكَ فَقُلْ أَنْعَمَ اَللَّهُ بَالَكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.298 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If your brother says to you after you come out of the bath, 'May your bath be pleasant,' then reply with, 'May Allah (swt) grant you well-being.'"

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.299 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"Disease is of three types, and the cure is of three types : As for the ailments, they are blood, bile, and phlegm. The cure for blood is with cupping, the cure for phlegm is the bathhouse, and the cure for bile is walking."
records.arabicText: 299 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «اَلدَّاءُ ثَلاَثَةٌ وَ اَلدَّوَاءُ ثَلاَثَةٌ فَأَمَّا اَلدَّاءُ فَالدَّمُ وَ اَلْمِرَّةُ وَ اَلْبَلْغَمُ فَدَوَاءُ اَلدَّمِ اَلْحِجَامَةُ وَ دَوَاءُ اَلْبَلْغَمِ اَلْحَمَّامُ وَ دَوَاءُ اَلْمِرَّةِ اَلْمَشِيُّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.299 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Disease is of three types, and the cure is of three types : As for the ailments, they are blood, bile, and phlegm. The cure for blood is with cupping, the cure for phlegm is the bathhouse, and the cure for bile is walking."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.300 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"There are three things that can weaken and potentially destroy the body: eating dried meat excessively, entering the bathhouse on a full stomach, and marrying an elderly woman."
It is also reported: "Intimacy on a full stomach."
records.arabicText:
300 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «ثَلاَثَةٌ يَهْدِمْنَ اَلْبَدَنَ وَ رُبَّمَا قَتَلْنَ أَكْلُ اَلْقَدِيدِ اَلْغَابِّ وَ دُخُولُ اَلْحَمَّامِ عَلَى اَلْبِطْنَةِ وَ نِكَاحُ اَلْعَجُوزِ.
وَ رُوِيَ: «اَلْغِشْيَانُ عَلَى اَلاِمْتِلاَءِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.300 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There are three things that can weaken and potentially destroy the body: eating dried meat excessively, entering the bathhouse on a full stomach, and marrying an elderly woman."
It is also reported: "Intimacy on a full stomach."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.301 - Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said: "Trimming the nails on Jumu’ah provides protection from leprosy, insanity, vitiligo, and blindness. If you do not need to trim them, then scrape or clean them."
records.arabicText: 301 - وَ رَوَى هِشَامُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ - «تَقْلِيمُ اَلْأَظْفَارِ يَوْمَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ يُؤْمِنُ مِنَ اَلْجُذَامِ وَ اَلْجُنُونِ وَ اَلْبَرَصِ وَ اَلْعَمَى فَإِنْ لَمْ تَحْتَجْ فَحُكَّهَا حَكّاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.301 - Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Trimming the nails on Jumu’ah provides protection from leprosy, insanity, vitiligo, and blindness. If you do not need to trim them, then scrape or clean them."

records.chapter: The Ritual Bath of jumu’ah, Entering the Bathhouse, Its Etiquette, and Teachings on Cleanliness and Beautification
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.302 - In another report, it is stated:
"If you do not need to trim them (your nails), then pass a knife or scissors over them."
records.arabicText: 302 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ: «فَإِنْ لَمْ تَحْتَجْ فَأَمِرَّ عَلَيْهَا اَلسِّكِّينَ أَوِ اَلْمِقْرَاضَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.302 - In another report, it is stated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you do not need to trim them (your nails), then pass a knife or scissors over them."
